title: Unsupported methods should not be called on some collection implementations
description: Some Java collection implementations do not support all operations. Calling unsupported methods on such collections will result in runtime exceptions, typically UnsupportedOperationException. This rule detects calls to methods that are not supported by certain collection types, helping to prevent unexpected failures at runtime.
recommendation: Avoid calling methods that are not supported by the specific collection implementation you are using. Refer to the Java documentation for the collection type to understand which operations are supported.
impact: Calling unsupported methods on collections can cause runtime exceptions, leading to application crashes or unexpected behavior.

examples.bad:
List<String> list = Arrays.asList("a", "b");
list.add("c"); // Throws UnsupportedOperationException
examples.good:
List<String> list = new ArrayList<>(Arrays.asList("a", "b"));
list.add("c"); // Works fine

checks.message: Avoid calling methods that are not supported by the specific collection implementation you are using. Refer to the Java documentation for the collection type to understand which operations are supported.
